text: Lesson 36
zh: 第36课

text: A chance in a million
zh: 百万分之一的机会

text: What was the chance in a million?
zh: 百万分之一的机会是什么呢？

text: We are less credulous than we used to be.
zh: 我们不像过去那样容易轻信了。

text: In the nineteenth century, a novelist would bring his story to a conclusion
zh: 在十九世纪，小说家常常通过一连串的巧合来收尾故事——

text: by presenting his readers with a series of coincidences--most of them wildly improbable.
zh: 这些巧合大多离奇得让人难以置信。

text: Readers happily accepted the fact that an obscure maidservant was really the hero's mother.
zh: 读者们欣然接受这样的情节：一个默默无闻的女仆竟然是主人公的母亲。

text: A long-lost brother, who was presumed dead, was really alive all the time
zh: 一个被认为早已死去、失散多年的哥哥其实一直活着，

text: and wickedly plotting to bring about the hero's downfall. And so on.
zh: 还在暗中策划让主人公身败名裂。诸如此类。

text: Modern readers would find such naive solutions totally unacceptable.
zh: 现代读者会觉得这种天真的情节安排完全无法接受。

text: Yet, in real life, circumstances do sometimes conspire to bring about coincidences which anyone but a nineteenth century novelist would find incredible.
zh: 然而在现实生活中，各种因素有时确实会凑到一起，制造出连十九世纪小说家都会觉得难以置信的巧合。

text: When I was a boy, my grandfather told me how a German taxi driver, Franz Bussman,
zh: 我小时候，祖父曾告诉我一个德国出租车司机弗朗茨·布斯曼的故事，

text: found a brother who was thought to have been killed twenty years before.
zh: 他找到了一个被认为二十年前就已经死去的哥哥。

text: While on a walking tour with his wife, he stopped to talk to a workman.
zh: 有一次他和妻子徒步旅行时，停下来跟一个工人搭话。

text: After they had gone on, Mrs. Bussman commented on the workman's close resemblance to her husband
zh: 他们继续往前走后，布斯曼太太提起那个工人长得跟丈夫很像，

text: and even suggested that he might be his brother.
zh: 甚至说他可能就是丈夫的哥哥。

text: Franz poured scorn on the idea,
zh: 弗朗茨对这个想法嗤之以鼻，

text: pointing out that his brother had been killed in action during the war.
zh: 并指出他哥哥早已在战争中阵亡。

text: Though Mrs. Bussman was fully acquainted with this story,
zh: 尽管布斯曼太太对这段往事一清二楚，

text: she thought that there was a chance in a million that she might be right.
zh: 她还是觉得，说不定百万分之一的可能性下自己是对的。

text: A few days later, she sent a boy to the workman to ask him if his name was Hans Bussman.
zh: 几天后，她派了个男孩去问那个工人，是不是叫汉斯·布斯曼。

text: Needless to say, the man's name was Hans Bussman and he really was Franz's long-lost brother.
zh: 不用说，那人果然叫汉斯·布斯曼，而且确实就是弗朗茨失散多年的哥哥。

text: When the brothers were reunited,
zh: 兄弟俩重逢时，

text: Hans explained how it was that he was still alive.
zh: 汉斯解释了自己是如何活下来的。

text: After having been wounded towards the end of the war,
zh: 战争快结束时他负了伤，

text: he had been sent to hospital and was separated from his unit.
zh: 被送进医院，从此与部队失去了联系。

text: The hospital had been bombed and Hans had made his way back into Western Germany on foot.
zh: 后来医院遭到轰炸，汉斯只好徒步返回西德。

text: Meanwhile, his unit was lost and all records of him had been destroyed.
zh: 与此同时，他所在的部队下落不明，他的所有记录也都被销毁了。

text: Hans returned to his family home, but the house had been bombed
zh: 汉斯回到家里，却发现房子已被炸毁，

text: and no one in the neighbourhood knew what had become of the inhabitants.
zh: 邻居们也不知道家人的下落。

text: Assuming that his family had been killed during an air raid,
zh: 他以为家人都已在空袭中遇难，

text: Hans settled down in a village fifty miles away where he had remained ever since.
zh: 于是就在五十英里外的一个村子里安顿下来，从此一直住在那里。
